Text:
Nothing works and too depressed to help myself 

im 28, a man, i was bullied a decade ago for no go reason such as just being too different and socially awkward, got depressed, ive sought help for the past decade,  
tried meds for years, didnt help, got tired of the side effects, especially the weight gain, i cant do it anymore  
ive seen probably close to 50 professionals, nothing helps  
i tried to do the things i like, it never helped  
peer support hasnt helped  
suicide lines havent helped

im exhausted and i cant do anything anymore. i see suicide as my only option. i cant be helped, i cant help myself. im here because i suffer every minute, i have no where to go, and i cant take it anymore

I need these bad thoughts out of my head

I can't anymore, I've been a overthinker ever since covid and it wasn't as bad now, with how bad my job even though I just got a raise and the boss is being very nice (I think he just wants me to stay) but they keep finding more ways to  wreck my mood. My friends, on the other hand, seem to be just pushing me away, I've told them that I'm not OK, I lied, telling one I'm seeing a therapist. Anyway, me and my friends haven't been good since I was 16. Just one bad experience after another and I was extremely lonely until I met them and I thought we were really close but all they seem to do is push me away recently,I had a anxiety attack twice in front of them, they did nothing put ignored me. Well, I just realized as I wrote that that's the negative thoughts. Idk if it's all in my head? Am I seeking attention? I hurt myself today, hitting my own head to "get the negative thoughts out." I'm afraid of where I'm going. I don't want my family to see me like this. I was never really comfortable going to them about my problems, just for them to tell me what everyone else said. I've tried journaling, distractions, eating less junk, focusing on work. It's only getting worse, I haven't had an attack for a while, but I was close today. Idk what anyone could tell me if you even read all this. I wouldn't. Thank you anyway

Feeling stuck

I, 22F, have always been an anxious person and have managed things well up until this point. Always anxious, awkward, insecure, and a “half glass empty” kind of person.

Lately, things have gotten worse in such little time. Work that I used to love and enjoy is so overwhelming. I feel like a burden to those around me. I am embarrassed to continue telling the few friends i have that i am still struggling and haven’t gotten better. I feel SO alone. 


Physically, I never feel well. I am sick so often and this on top of that has me so drained. I have no motivation, numb to the positive things but the negatives are so heavy. 

How do I continue like this? Is there a “better”? Or do i just learn how to manage?

Hard to get motivated

So, I've had the realization over the last month that I don't know when I was last truly happy. I have a wife and kids, love them and love being around them even if just sitting around the house. However, my issue is I can't find anything that I actually care about. The things that used to entertain me have become dull, boring, and often a chore to even consider doing. I love golfing (even when I suck), but now all I think about when considering going out is the cost and the time away from my family which makes me just not do it. 
I can't find a single thing that I'm interested in doing anymore which means I sit around generally feeling bad for myself. I'm on meds, adjusting the dosage now that I've realized what's going on, but now I'm just more clear headed and it's even easier to see how little I care for any activity or subject. I'm damn near 35 years old and I don't have a hobby that I care about. Really hard to try to go back to school like I've wanted when I can't think of a single thing I'd want to do with my life. It's hard to "follow your passion" when there's nothing you're interested in. 

At this point I feel like I've finished the movie and I'm just waiting for the credits to finish rolling. I feel like someone out there has probably been here before. Any ideas to help me get motivated to get out of bed rather than being annoyed that I woke up again would be great.
Cause: mental health struggle and anxiety disorder

Text:
How do I calm myself down from anxious thoughts about someone I’m talking to 

I’ve been talking to a woman, we’re both unsure what’s happening and our friends don’t know we talk to each other. 

She’s on a trip with friends and I’ve been an anxious wreck. Her messages are delayed since she doesn’t want to seem like she’s always on her phone, and I too asked her to take it easy and reply when she can, enjoy the trip. 

It’s making me feel like crap, I constantly check my phone, I keep on wanting and waiting to see her messages, I reread whatever she sends me and the communication is very minimal. I don’t know if it’s warm since it’s text based but the conversation does seem dryer than normal. 

There’s like a knot or weight on my chest and I’m lashing out sometimes. I’m just so angry and I don’t know how to manage this. 

I feel like I’m being abandoned or that she is talking to someone else, and I can’t really be sure if either of the things are true. And tbh I also need to get ready for heartbreak if she’s gonna stop talking with me after the trip. 

I hate how I feel and I just want to feel better - please suggest something to help me. I don’t know how I will work through this.
Cause: lack of control, over reliance on messaging , uncertainty in relationship

Text:
I’m just tired and want a break

Sorry for formatting I’m on mobile. 

I’m in my 20’s, a college dropout and work a job I just hate. I have plans and am in the works of starting a career I think will be fulfilling but I’m tired of waiting around. 

I’m tired of social interactions feeling like a chore, I have friends but I don’t want to be around them. I’ve been in many relationships but when it gets too real I leave. I keep thinking I’m gonna die alone because anyone I’m interested in, I stay dry and don’t let them in. 

I’m tired of feeling like nothing matters and the world’s gonna go to shit anyway so why care. 

I’m tired of keeping up appearances so nobody asks if anything is going on. 

I’m active in aa, the desire and cycle has left until recently and I’m tired of being too myself to tell anyone. 

I’m tired of imagining I wrap my car around a tree any time I drive, or wish I had a fast motorcycle so I could just let go. 

I’m tired of finding it impossible to be venerable and let people in when I need help, I’ve done therapy a few times and I couldn’t tell them what was really going on in my head. 

I’m tired of controlling my negative impulses, like refraining from driving off a bridge, sinking my boat offshore, you get the picture. 

I wouldn’t say I’m suicidal but man I just want a break. 

I’m just tired and want to rest.

I don’t know what to do 

It’s gotten to the point that I’m doing cuts to my dermis every day now and I’m scared that someone will notice, I’m so fucking scared, not really from the self harm, but about how this is going to go. I’m too scared to tell my councillor or my mother. I’ve had so much going on and I just need fucking help. 


I wish my dad would fucking understand he just doesn’t give a fuck. And the law in nz fucking sucks, someone who was a LITERAL CHILD HEALTH SPECIALIST SAID THAT THEY DIDNT CARE UNLESS IT WAS PHYSICAL. I need help so bad and I have no fucking idea what to do, this is kinda a vent but also a call for help and advice. Please help I don’t want this to get worse.

Toxic magnet 

I don’t know what it is. The last slew of jobs I’ve held have all had toxic environments. I’ve done my best to help change that around and provide a voice for everyone within the workplaces I have been in and it’s always to my detriment. 

I attract toxic people in general. I’m just trying to make sure everyone is okay but I’m always the one that suffers for it. How do I stop doing this? I’ve basically limited myself to social interactions with certain individuals and cut ties with people who have had a negative impact on me but I still find it hard to find or even trust solid people. 

I’m very burnt out in every aspect of my life.
Cause: empathy and people pleasing, lack of support

I'm addicted to cheating

The title is a pretty good start. I'm addicted to cheating and it's killing me inside. 

I think it first started during my first relationship. I only had a couple relationships in my later years of highschool and in one my girlfriend ended up leaving me when I left for rehab. She ultimately ended up hooking up with my friend, which obviously hurt pretty bad. My next girlfriend cheated on my via text, but as far as I know she never physically cheated on me. Even the texts weren't horrible, no nudes or anything, just very flirtatious and slightly sexually charged messages. I should also probably mention that in my chase for both of these girls, I was friends with them before dating and I knew they were very promiscuous when single. Even that turned me on for some reason. 

  
From these experiences, I developed a slight cuckolding kink. That kink slowly transformed into fantasizing about my girlfriend cheating on me. This is where things started to go off the rails. I became somewhat obsessed with the ENTIRETY of cheating. Homewrecking, succumbing to a homewrecker, my girlfriend cheating on me, etc. I feel like a piece of shit everytime I orgasm to these thoughts andmy porn addiction definitely doesn't help. 



In the midst of all that, I ended up hooking up with my friends crush and potential "girlfriend". He's my best friend to this day but the rush and thrill of not getting caught made me feel amazing. She would hook up with him then text me asking to hook up and I'd always say yes. 

  
One time during a hookup the guilt got so bad I left in the middle, but texted her meet back up later that night.

  
I've since gone to college and am now in a relationship of about 8 months. I'm still struggling with these urges and want nothing more than to get rid of them.



It's like a thought I can't get out of my head. I think about it at least once a day but usually more. Usually once I masturbate the thoughts go away but just a couple hours after it'll be back on my mind. 



I've never physically cheated on my partner, although I made plans to meet up with the partner I mentioned previously. I never followed through on them, despite her agreeing to hook up.

  
I know I'm a piece of shit, I don't need anyone to tell me that. I just want to know how I can get better.

Job Problems

*For context, I suffer from severe ASD and/or OCD (waiting assessment)*

I was fired illegally from a job I loved at the end of July, which has sent me on a downward spiral of panic attacks and muscle constrictions and the worst of all, job hopping. 

A couple weeks after I was fired, I got a job at a factory. I had to leave before my first day was even over due to a blood pressure condition I have. This upset me as I loved the stability and working hours the factory offered.  

After this I got a job at a gas station and started on Monday. I managed to get through my first shift, and my next one was not until today. In the two days between shifts I was in a constant state of panic and borderline delirium, and got to call a crisis team twice. I then made the decision to quit the gas station as the shift pattern triggered my anxiety. 

I have suffered greatly due to my mental health conditions, and now other people are suffering too. I am trying to get help but as I’m in the UK, it could be years. 

Thank you all for listening to my rant, and I welcome any advice or support

Help with letting go of a breakup.


It’s my first post here and I’m not sure if it’s the right place but someone directed me to this page and I’m hoping there’s a counsellor or therapist that’s able to reply.

Basically I’ve ended a relationship and my ex is a high functioning addict and alcoholic. Also reading up a lot on avoidant attachments makes me wonder if it was an attachment issue or was it his drug and alcohol usage that makes him incapable of having a relationship. I ask this because I’m sober and don’t know how to reconcile him with the imagine I have of someone who’s an addict. 

I’m having problems letting go and am in a lot of pain currently. I can’t focus on task at hand and feel numb and like a zombie. 

I keep ruminating and stalking his socials I’ve since deleted them and removed him as a follower because I don’t want to attach any meaning to his viewing my stories and neither do I want to be affected by his posts/stories. 

I’ve tried searching for al-anon but I’m in Asia and my country is a zero drug tolerance country so I don’t have much of a support there.

Everytime do sometimes to help move on an heal I feel like I’m being sucked back into the heartache. Like recently deleting his number so I’ll never be able to know when he was last seen, while this is to help me move forward it made me feel so much pain and I felt like I lost him all over again. 

In avoidant attachment this is called reactivating strategy like a form of control.

Can someone guide me on how do I heal my wounds that attach me to him or if it’s a chemical/hormonal imbalance I’m experiencing cause I feel like he’s the drug or the feeling of pain I felt from the relationship was my drug.

If it helps he was mainly using weed, and when he can’t smoke up, he would take lean or codine and sleeping pills. His grandfather died of alcohol poisoning and his father drinks a lot as well. His friends and sisters also smoke weed. He previously got very depressed he jumped of the 2nd or 3 story of a building when he stopped smoking weed.

I don’t I only drink occasionally and I think I’m a secure anxious attachment or at least that’s what the answer I got from the questionnaire.

my mind is slipping into that dark place again

Toes curled tight on the ledge’s brink,  
A whispering void, darker than we think.  
The sky stretches wide, but the world feels small,  
Caught between flight and that endless fall.

The wind tugs, inviting you near,  
Its sings escape; but echoes fear.  
The heart beats fast, a desperate plea,  
For someone to notice, for someone to see.

Beneath the silence, a battle rages—  
A mind trapped in invisibly dark cages.  
Yet standing here on the edge, toes hanging free,  
You weigh the weight of what you might be.

But below the abyss, there's more than just air,  
There are voices, hands, and hearts that care.  
And though you dangle, so close to the end,  
There’s still time to find your way again.

So I hold on tight, for one more today—  
For dawn is the light of the softest way.  
And though the edge is all you can see,  
The path from this, is waiting quietly.

I am deteriorating

I'm and 18 year old male. Freshmen in college. My mental health is deteriorating. The only reason I'm doing stuff is because I have nothing else to do. Some background, I moved 2 years ago across country. I have never been great at making friends. I have clue how to initate conversations and have it develop into a friendship. However I do have a decent online following where I text and talk with some online friends. But I'm coming to realize online friends no matter how good they are will never supplement in-person face to face interaction. I am a college freshman. I just graduated high school and so far life has been a rollercoaster. The summer was actually great. I went on a trip with some people I've never met before for 2 weeks. I got super close with this one girl who lives 2 hours away. This was my first face to face in person friendgroup I have ever been comfortable around. But of course the trip ended and we went our seperate ways. I have talked to this girl the most. She's awesome to talk to. I really do love her. I am not sure how interested she is in me though. Before I was almost certain we both had a mutual liking for each other. What I'm about to say is a main reason why my mental health is bad. Because the person who I feel the most comfortable around is hard to reach out to and so difficult to figure out. So let me just say after the trip ended, I was supposed to go spend the day with her. That fell through. She told me why and I understood. I didn't really think much of it. She moved to college shortly after that. Now she is also impossible to reach out to. Her phone is on DND for most of the day and she gets overwhelmed by notifications so she just swipes and sometimes doesn't respond at all. For someone who I was almost sure took interest in me to just not respond didn't sit right with me. She has clarified it's nothing personal but idk. It doesn't really sit right. Of course I respect her boundaries and decisions but I am almost certain we would be great together. But she is now in college and I don't(I shouldn't as well) know who she is talking to. She may be talking to a ton of guys right now. Again, none of my business but when she is far away and impossible to reach out to it makes it extra difficult and easy for me to overthink. Now let's go back to me for a minute. I was a good high school student. But I didn't get accepted to any of my college preferences. I am now attending a school with no housing and a 100% acceptance rate. I was already upset with that because I hold myself to a higher standard. I wanted to move out more than anything and it hurt that all the work I put in high school didn't pay off at all. I'm still living at home. I guarantee you I wouldn't have these problems if I was living in a dorm. It's SO MUCH EASIER to meet people and I want to meet people. Instead I'm just passing by hoping to get into the same schools but as a transfer for the spring semester. This unhappiness is taking a toll on me. I already worked really hard once and got nothing in return. Subconsciously I'm thinking even if I do great this semester I won't get accepted in any of these schools. Now I'm starting to fall behind which makes it worse. Saying it out loud here actually already helps but I know it's just temporary. This college has no community. It's made for students to transfer out so nobody cares to get to know each other and I hate that. I have also applied to almost anywhere possible to get a job to make money and have more in-person interactions. But this job market sucks and half the places I apply to don't even care to remove open positions from their website or job listing apps when they're filled. For the time I'm stuck at a college I didn't want to be at in the first place, I have no way to connect with anyone here, I am procrastinating from initial disappointment, and the girl I love being around the most has me overthinking everything. I'm starting to become lost. I usually don't open up but I want to want to get help before this hole becomes deeper than it already is.

Been Through My Share of Trauma This Year

This year has been really hard for me. I was laid off for a few months, and that really knocked the wind out of my sails. I had been at my job a few years and had a routine and stuff. I’m working again but I haven’t gotten back on my feet mentally so to speak. I was also in a toxic romantic situation for a few months (manipulative and emotionally abusive) and that also really affected me. I finally had the courage to break things off with the person this past weekend, and while I’m happy to finally be out of the situation, there is a void in my life as a result A lot of my friends are busy and I don’t really feel like I have a lot of people to open up to right now. 

I welcome any words of advice or thoughts or even new connections. Maybe there are other people who are struggling who feel alone or wish they had more connections in their life. Maybe there are people who can relate to what I’ve been to.

I'm so alone and unloved and I don't think there's anything to do to help that.

I started college recently. I'd been working on myself over summer after the break up of a 2 year relationship, trying to look good and take better care of myself so I'd look more approachable and/or attractive. I just wanted to make friends because I know I have very few, maybe even talk to some girls. But the effort was for nothing, I'm so lonley, no one wants to talk to me. People I try to talk to treat me like some weirdo and those that I have gotten along with just ignore me now. Girls couldn't be less interested, I don't even care about relationships anymore I just want to make friends. I'm an afterthought. I was sat in my psychology today and I was the only person sat alone in a classroom that on paper shouldn't be able to fit everyone. A guy I thought I made friends with pulled the chair next to me out then pushed it back on when he saw someone else, the girl who I had spoken to abit ignored me and went to sit next to some other guy and as I sat there feeling so incredibly sad all the suicidal thoughts rushed in. And the terrifying thing is that they made me feel better. No one cares that I'm here, so why would anyone care when I'm gone. All I want is someone to be friends with, someone who likes me for me. Everywhere I look I see groups of friends laughing or happy couples and it kills me. Everyone else has those things, yet here I am so horribly alone. No one wants to chat to me, let alone love me. I just want someone I can talk to when everything gets too much, someone who genuinely cares about me, not because I'm related to them or because they're a teacher or a student counsellor, someone who likes me because of who I am. And no one does. I guess I just don't deserve that. I don't see any other option.

I’m really hoping I can turn my mental health around the next few months…

So last year in April my mom passed away. I was absolutely distraught and already suffering from undiagnosed panic disorder, depression and ADHD. 

I did do ketamine therapy which helped tremendously and TMS. Also got diagnosed with the above illnesses as well as PTSD. Starting ADHD medication helped a LOT and for the last few months of 2023 I actually felt…good? Despite the circumstances. I barely took the clonazepam my psych prescribed, maybe 1-2 times a month at most. I actually felt optimistic about life despite having no family and going through something so traumatic.

Ocean of emotions 


September 04, 2024

 A wave of sadness 

Do you ever feel like everything is going fine and all of a sudden there is a wave of sadness. Where everything seems fine but nothing is.

There is no scientific name for this phenomenon but it can be often referred to Emotional Flash, Affective wave, Sadness Attack etc.

All these things summaries to the uncontrollable sadness which we suddenly feel.

And these things are not very easy to make people understand especially your family because perhaps they never felt this way so it is not easy for them to comprehend, and even if they try after some recurring episodes they will find it difficult to see or understand how you feel when you share this. 

They might even say you just want attention or you are just overthinking and then and there it feels like true they are not responsible for the way you are feeling so there it no solution.

In this world it is very difficult to make a social life and even if you achieve it sometimes this wave hits you hard. If we go to a professional they will try to correlate this with depression, bipolar disorder, anxiety disorder, or even ptsd. Now I am not qualified to comment on that but there can be many other reasons to

Now there comes a very simple follow up question. Why do we feel the sudden wave of sadness?

To be honest I don't have an answer which might support any assumption.

There can be many reasons to feel this way. But first we have to understand that emotions are unpredictable they can be like a surprise visit of an old friend reminding the nostalgia.

• One thing can be Sometimes Life's Just to much so even when things are good it can be overwhelming. 

• Second thing to remember is Memory lingers whether it is good or bad. 

• Third thing is we need a release. Sometimes we keep so many thing suppressed knowingly or unknowingly that memories or emotions are bound to resurface. 

Because you didn't dealt with the emotions at that time they might just pop up unannounced when you don't expect or you absolutely forgot.

There are some simple tricks which you should keep under your sleeves like:

1. Allow yourself to feel 

2. Connect with yourself

3. Reach out to someone 

4. Do journaling 

5. Remind yourself things will pass

∆ These tricks can or cannot work for you, you need to explore yourself and see what works best. 

And if you still feel the persistent sadness seek out help from professional. There is no shame in that. 



•∆ Remember it is okay to feel sad sometimes even when there is nothing wrong, it is part and parcel of life.
                                                  -  Strothwrites

Am I in the wrong for thinking this way?

I, for once, want to be truly seen and cared for. I always, and I mean ALWAYS, put others before me, but I just want someone, ANYONE, to put me first.

I've always been the type of person who goes out of their way to help others. Whether it's family, friends, or even coworkers, I'm the one who listens, offers advice, and provides support whenever it's needed. I genuinely care about the well-being of those around me, and it brings me joy to see them happy. I am always there to lend an ear and for them to vent to.

I've always been that kind of person; everyone knows me as that kind of person. That was how I was raised—to tune into others' emotions, gauge how they would react, and just please them to avoid getting rejected or pushed out. That's how, as a middle child, I was raised.

But lately, I've been feeling so lonely. I realized I don't have anyone to reach out to—not to vent, not to just sit and talk in silence. And nobody thought to reach out and check on me, even though I have been silent for multiple days now.

**Past Friendships:**

I used to be friends with these girls back in high school, really close-knit, and then I realized that I might be the odd one out—the one who never understood the joke, the extra. It wasn't always like that, but when we started going to university, it became really obvious. So I dropped them—not in the best way, but it already happened.

**Current Friend Group:**

Then I found this incredible group I thought I could really connect to. We were the trio who always had each other's backs. Five years strong now, until I realized the same pattern is happening.

**MY Love Language:**

So hear me out. I might be a materialistic person, but that's my love language, I think. I love giving people gifts, even when I am really poor. I always find some way to spend money on them and make them feel nice. But I never really received the best gifts—cheap pairs of Dollar Tree headsets, poorly factory-made teddy bears, some candy, cheap candles. But I still accepted them anyways because at least they tried, somewhat.

If it ever was handmade, I would be over the moon because I know how much time it would take, and I would cherish it. However, that never happened.

**Current Dilemma:**

Anyways, my dilemma right now with this group of friends: Every year on both of their birthdays, I always try my hardest to coordinate with our third best friend to make the other girl's birthday a nice surprise or gift. I would be willing to spend a lot of money on decorations, cake, the perfect gift—something that they need and want that we worked hard to make sure is perfect. I spent even more money during the period when we lived together for a year. Sadly, my birthday passed before we moved in (two weeks before), and we never got to throw a party for me, but we did throw two parties for the other two girls.

**Last Year's Birthday:**

It's been a year since we moved out. Last year, they sent me an Amazon package to my door when it was my birthday. It was a noodle bowl. It was cute; it had little kitties on it, but it was still something that I won't be using much. They bought it because I liked noodles..., before that I received a eyeshadow pallet that I already had from Amazon and makeup brushes that were pretty decent for Amazon but were so harsh it made my sensitive skin break out. 

For context, for their birthdays, we got one a lovely perfume in her favorite scent, plus some candles and trinkets. The other we got an expensive natural oil diffuser, plus a table keyboard mat and noise-canceling earbuds (for her sensitivity issues). Both of them still use their products till now, while I have mine in a cupboard untouched.

**Upcoming Birthday:**

Now that another year has passed, it's time for my birthday in less than two weeks, and I have a very, very, very strong feeling that I won't be getting much this year. I can already tell—one will be too busy with work, and the other will be busy with her parents. I just know that I will receive another Amazon package at my doorstep.

**Conclusion:**

What do I do in this situation? I am tired of being an afterthought. I don't know what I am doing wrong. I don't know what to do with myself at this point. I feel guilty for not appreciating what they've given me because what if they meant well and thought they were great ideas. But then I keep thinking about how I have already told them about my previous friend group and why I broke up with them, and how gift giving is my love language and that I do my best to find the perfect gift at a reasonable price. Am I just too materialistic? Do I need to be the one to change??

  
I apologize for the weird format, I'm not in the best mental state and I had chat gpt do its best in organizing my thoughts. Anyways, I hope you all have an incredible day/night.

Hi! I am 34 years old. I'm autistic, has ADHD, and has a visual disabilities such as myopia and Retinopathy of Prematurity. I am diagnosed with depression, anxiety, and PTSD.

I would like to share my story with you all. I am seeking words of encouragement and comfort, and advices as well. Please be mindful of the comments and please don't judge. 🥺

I was born a 6 months prematured infant, thus the cause of my inborn disabilities. I was also an illegitimate child born out of wedlock from "the other woman" which I wouldn't know until I'm 18 years old. 

Since I was a child, I often get bullied by the people around me for being abnormal. They teased me for being "duling" even if it's only "banlag". I was often called abnormal, weird, crazy, baliw, mongoloid, autistic, nerd, etc. by people around me, be it teachers, classmates, family, and neighbors. At first I couldn't understand why people seem to hate me. I can walk, talk, and do things like them, even if I was wearing specially-made thick eyeglasses. As I grow up, I find people picking on me due to my odd hobbies, such as watching anime, drawing and writing. My imagination was quite wild and I'm quite creative. I never actually thought that watching anime was bad, but it's one reason why people call me abnormal, especially when it comes to my fixation with a certain anime character who I liked since I'm 6 years old even until now.

Still, I never thought of it as a bad thing. What was different from being a fan of those popular actors or singers? I'm not the only one who watches anime. I'm not the only one who has this character as her/his favorite character of all time. I don't see anything bad about my reading, writing and drawing hobbies.

Aside from that, I remember being very angry and impatient as a child. I throw tantrums whenever I feel overwhelmed by certain emotions such as rage, sadness or grief. I also have the tendency to throw things around me in anger, because I cannot control myself. Loud noises also makes me feel greatly annoyed and triggers explosive anger or weakness in me whenever it feels too uncomfortable. I remember my mother would often scold me for being rude whenever she had her noisy [and annoying to me] guests come over. People hated me for that, and even until now, I still have the tendency to throw tantrums or what my family thinks is an "exaggerated act" whenever I get overwhelmed by noise, large crowds of people, or my emotions. It's really hard. 

One day when I was in high school, my Math teacher called my mother to school to talk about my behavior. I remember she told my mom "Nixie is always spacing out during class. It's as if she's in her own world. She doesn't focus well and is hardly socializing with her classmates due to her being bullied by them." and she asked my mom if I was ever gotten any check ups from a psychiatrist. My mom tried to consider but my dad refused because he believed that he doesn't have a "special child".

Growing up, I have very few friends. I struggled everyday, due to both my visual disabilities and whatever behavioral problem I have. My mother would often scold me for being immature, which I couldn't understand. (She must be thinking about my fixations and hobbies or how I act and move too). Other people would also tell me "That's why people call you abnormal because of how you act. Stop being childish and act like a normal person! Be matured!" which, to this day, I wasn't able to do, thus leading to them thinking I'm just being stubborn or egotistical for "not trying to change" (believe me, I tried, but I couldn't).

So when I was already 18 years old, my aunt revealed to me the truth about me being illegitimate and that my parents are adulterers, which lead me to becoming cursed by God by a generational curse and that I am not allowed to go to heaven because of it. That revelation shook me to the core, and I felt anger towards my parents, and especially, more towards myself.

I hated myself.

My performance in school became terrible to the point I failed. My father halted my studying for two years, thinking that I wasn't studying well when it's the emotional turmoil that affected my ability to focus that has affected me greatly. I also isolated myself from the world. 

My mom and dad broke up after that. And I was left feeling even more devastated. 

When I tried finding a job, I was told by my uncle that the reason why the HR in their office didn't hire me was because they asked him if I was a mongoloid. My uncle was ashamed about it and told my mom to throw away my collection and works. My mom challenged him to do it himself and see how I would react, and if ever I "go into a state of shock or rampage, he should do something to calm me down and bring me back". My mom revealed to me that she did that a few years before and what happened was I went into a state of 'lifelessness' as she called it, and had the biggest tantrum she saw me did. I don't remember that much about the incident though and now that I think about it, that's quite embarrassing.

So after that incident, I asked my dad if he thinks I'm abnormal too. If I was mongoloid, or whatever was wrong with me. I asked him if I should go and see a psychiatrist because I do need help. He refused because he said he doesn't believe that.

I asked to study again. He allowed me to do so. My mom died during that time, and it took a toll on me. I did my best to study, and yet, just as in any other school I've attended, I got bullied here as well, and worst, even by the Dean herself. I was isolated and discriminated by everyone for the reason I wasn't hygienic enough for them. (Idk why, I do take a bath, brush my teeth, etc. but it has something to do with a strong body odor which I wasn't even aware of. That and my disabilities too. I did what I could to be more hygienic, to rid to the bad odor, but I have no idea if it's even effective or not because I couldn't even tell). 

My mom died during this time, and I was left with no one. My aunt took me in though [as long as my dad provides me his support]. And because of these circumstances, 6 months because I graduated, I lost it.

I went into a state of shock. I just felt like I couldn't take it any longer. The grief, the loneliness, the pain, the bullying, the disgusted words and discrimination... I was send to the ER for "just spacing out and being frigid and silent for 5 hours straight". The doctors suggested my dad to take me to a psychiatrist.

That's how I was diagnosed with depression, anxiety, and PTSD. That was year 2014, ten years ago. I was also suggested that I take up a test for ASD and ADHD because I was showing up signs of it, but my dad refused because it's very expensive.

My dad was furious at me. He called me "nag-iinarte" (acting up exaggeratedly), that I didn't need to see this doctor, and he said that he was hoping that I don't need any medicines at all. The cost also made him livid as one session costs around 4000php at the time. It was discontinued after I got kicked out of the school because I wasn't attending anymore due to me just wanting to stay in my room in shame all day, isolating myself and self-blaming for being like this.

I wasn't able to graduate after all the hard work I did in studying. And now, it still affects me to this day because I can't find a proper and stable job not just because I wasn't able to finish my studies, but also because of my disabilities and mental health problems!

I worked several jobs, and due to my autism and ADHD (which was still undiagnosed to this day), had a hard time keeping it. I couldn't focus, I couldn't see properly, I have short-term memory too. I have a hard time socializing with my fellow co-workers, it feels awkward because everytime I do, they would also call me weird, abnormal, etc. I get scolded by superiors for "not focusing". Even if I try to explain, nobody wants to be patient with someone like me with lots of problems. 😭 If only there was someone who could give me that chance and patience... I would LOVE to work.

But then, my family doesn't understand at all. After my dad had a stroke, I wasn't able to see him again. My sisters won't let me visit too because according to them, "he doesn't want to see me". Both sides of my family think that whenever I try to explain my situation, that I'm just making up excuses to be lazy. It makes me feel frustrated. I want to live normally too, to be independent, to be able to work and earn money so I can pay my bills, rent, live on my own, and buy whatever food and things I want, without depending on anyone. But because of my disabilities and how people around me see and treat me, I am STRUGGLING to do that. It's not that I do NOT want to. I have lots of toys and books I wanted to buy with my own hard-earned money too. But people only say that I'm using my disabilities as an excuse. They don't understand. No, they don't WANT to understand. 😭

My 4th job was 2021, it took me 3 years to find another, and found one last June 2024. BUT THEN ANOTHER PROBLEM CAME IN. As I mentioned, I also have a visual disability. This caused me to be blinded by bright lights. Due to myopia, astigmatism, and ROP, I am having a hard time reading (hence why I use TTS to listen and STT to type). When I was a child, I had this hope that my vision will be better if I get LASIK surgery in the future. In my first job, I was bullied by my boss so my dad went with me to the hospital to have the surgery, but the doctors shattered my hope when they said I couldn't take it due to a possible retinal detachment. Yes, even as a child I was told I would get blind in the future, but the LASIK surgery was my only hope. If we proceeded, there is a high change I would become blind instantly. So my dad refuse for me to undergo the surgery. He thought it would be better for me to go blind slowly instead of instantly after all. So now, after realizing that the ROP has formed cataracts within my eyes and that my vision won't improve even if we put on higher grades for the glasses... My new job, within just me being employed there for 4 days, suggested that I resign (yeah, they witnessed how I struggle in work, maybe they were just concerned).

Of course, my family didn't take this lightly. Again, they called me out for "not acting normally" (I can't see well so I had to lean over whatever small things I was trying to read but still can't! How can I act like a normal person?), for being hopeless. They even said, "How are you going to pay for the surgery if you don't have any money? How can you earn money for that if you don't have a job?! Don't depend on us because we have nothing to spare for you! Don't expect on dad as well, he has no more money!"

My aunt's family, who took me after my mother died, also wants me out now because her daughter is getting married by October and they cannot bring me with them because "You are not our obligation anymore".

The rest of the family on both sides won't take me in either, simply because they dislike me for my disabilities. They see me as a problem, a deadweight, and no one is available to take care of me. They even blocked me on social media after knowing that. They even told my cousins to block me and never talked to me again. 

And to think that they are all Christians. Some are pastors too. They treated me as the devil, a cursed person, a Satanist. They don't want me because of that as well. 

I have no will to live any longer. Everyone has abandoned me. I feel utterly hopeless now.

Update -
*me whenever I try to explain to them. I show them the messages, posts, or videos explaining behaviors and symptoms of people with Autism and ADHD, wanting to tell my family how much I can relate to them.*

My family: *turns away* We don't know anything about that. And we're not interested.

They would appear to listen to others, but they still refuse to. Acknowledge it. They instead blame me for being a spoiled brat, or my parents for not raising me right. 

Seriously, if they were truly concerned or love me, they would do what it takes to understand. But they refuse to participate because according to them, "it's not our responsibility". Mabuti pa yung pastor na kakilala nila, mas may concern pa sa akin kaysa sa kanilang kadugo ko.

Hi everyone,

I'm reaching out because my Mum (trans male, they/them pronouns - has been on HRT for 1.5yrs)  has been going through a really tough time, and I'm not sure what to do. We are located in Australia.

Last Wednesday, they came home from work early and haven't been talking much since. They’ve been acting really weird—super quiet, either sitting/lying on the couch or in their bedroom with the door closed. They’ve been crying a lot, grunting, and sometimes hitting themselves in the head multiple times. They did this in front of my brother (16), son (2) and me in the car last Tuesday, which was very awkward.

They went into work on Friday morning and said they’re taking this week off. I think they went to a doctor’s appointment to talk about seeing a psychologist, but I’m not 100% sure. They really haven’t been talking much, and when they do, it’s under their breath, so I have to ask them to repeat what they said multiple times. I told them I’m worried about them and don’t know what to do, and they just said the same thing.

They’re also paranoid and think people are following them. They believe someone is trying to make them “seem crazy” through their phone. They won’t listen to me when I tell them it’s not real, and they’re convinced that someone’s out to get them and put them in prison.

They started a new job three weeks ago (after having being fired from they’re previous job 3months ago -unsure about the reason why) and has already taken this week off because they’re not in the right mental state.

Recently, they have been physically lashing out at my 16yo brother—restraining him and threatening to beat him up. I’m really worried about his safety.

This situation has been getting progressively worse over the last six months. I have tried talking to them about getting help, but they responded with, “I know you think I’m crazy, but just trust me.”

I called the local mental health crisis team, and they said they couldn’t do anything unless they voluntarily go or if we need to call the police, and told me to contact the local hospital. When I called the hospital, they told me to contact the crisis team. I’m feeling stuck and not sure what to do next.

What should I do to ensure their safety and the safety of my brother? Any advice would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

Summer 2024 thoughts

Hi. My name is Lucas, I'm 20 yo, currently studying in university. I don't really know how to do this, so it might get a little convoluted. I apologize in advance. Also, not a native english speaker, all of my vocabulary comes from movies, comics and videogames so I apologize if I sound overly dramatic.

A little data about me: I'm a very laid back person. I don't usually think badly of people which has gotten me into some problems for being the devil's advocate, sometimes I'll admit I was a pushover. Hell, I care too much avout what other people think of me. I have also been a very "smart" kid, with good marks all around and very well behaved. A grandmother's dream, so to speak. I have never really studied seriously for any exam since I was 6 (the negative numbers were my nemesis haha). But ever since then it has been "easy". Which made the transition into university a lot harder. This lazy lifestyle made me prioritize staying at home playing videogames instead of hanging out with friends outside (I live far away from populated areas, with no public transportation available, so up until mow I relied on my parents taking me there by a 30 minute car ride). That lifestyle is still very much present in my mind, even though I do not like it, because the world outside my room has a lot to offer and I want to try it. But everytime I try, my body feels so numb and my mind so insecure. I have also never had someone with whom I could speak 100% transparently, probably my own fault as you will see later.



I've been on a downward trajectory for about 9 or so  years now. As far as I can tell, there have been no triggers. No one close to me has ever passed away, I haven't had any major illnesses... My family is the most structured one, I have loving parents and a brother who has always been there for me. And yet, I have grown sad and introverted, which makes me feel worse because I feel that I'm making this all up.

The last time I remember feeling true, unbound childlike happiness was 6 years ago, during a school trip. Before that I had been increasingly sad, or rather numb. I even thought of killing myself, but from a child's perspective, who doesn't really comprehend what it is really about. I think it was just a way of putting into words or rather thoughts the void I was feeling at that age (when I was around 8 yo my best friend, the one I trusted the most and with whom I did everything left me for another friend group without previous notice). After that I changed schools because the school I studied at was a corrupt shithole and some time passed. I made new friends, although we never really connected on the same level. I have always been a shy person. I get embarrassed quickly and shut myself, so I have a hard time creating new relationships whenever I'm in a new environment, but this time I was lucky and found a group relatively quickly. Due to this shy nature I have never been a good conversation partner at first. I take months and months to grow comfortable enough with someone to maintain a serious talk. Because of this I decided to start taking a different approach. From this point onward I focused on being funny. You know, cracking jojes and making funny comments. From a child's perspective it looked promising, the funny guy is the smart guy right? I started taking this approach and soon enough it felt natural. This itself shoudn't be a problem, but with years of perpetually practicing it, it has kind of become my only way of communicating. It is very frustrating talking to someone who is being genuine with you and all you know how to do is being cynical and sarcastic. Not even in an aggressive way, just in a insincere one. Standing there, wanting to say what you really think and not knowing how to, only to end up resorting to the same crappy joke as always. This, combined with my natural shyness has turned me into a sort of jokes vending machine, who stands silent in a corner and when you interact with it, it releases a crappy joke and returns to silent mode. And it makes me sick everytime I do it  because I know there are other ways. But everytime I blank and don't know what to say.

This inability to be 100% genuine has prevented me from keeping friendships I treasured and also from developing new ones. This has made it really hard to express myself to the point where I don't know if the people close to me really know me, as I don't express my opinions or likings often. It is also hard to give and receive compliments or critiques, as I do not want to offend anyone. This is one of the things that has troubled me the most, because I think it might be the first step to take to get to a better way of living.

I don't know how to introduce this next point so here I go: girls. By the way I'm cis and hetero. I am not afraid of talking to them or anything like that, at least on a conscious level. In fact, most of my friend groups have been around 70% female and 30% male. If anything, I might have developped a general social anxiety problem.  You might think that texting would be easier, but somehow it's harder. Since I got late to the smartphone party because my parents didn't want my brother and I growing up with them (a decision I respect) I ended up really lost in the digital world. I didn't know any of the social cues, gestures or habits that make up a nice online person. Add to this the pressure of filling out the funny guy role and having to respond with a witty text every time and you get an awkward mess that doesn't answer often to messages. In my lowest point I got to not answering a single text in a whole summer. A real dick move. To be honest it was one of my worst periods of time.

 Anyway, despite all this, I think i have developped a kind of fear of relationships. I'll explain this. I don't consider myself especially good-looking. I have never cared about my looks much (I'm starting to and it's helping a bit with my self image) but somehow always managed to have some girl liking me. I really hope that didn't sound rude, I don't know how to make it sound more natural, sorry. Anyways, whenever someone would confess to me I would start panicking. I would think that I would never fulfill their expectations. That somehow I had tricked them into "falling in love" with an unreal, superior version of me. Stuff like that. I always ended up rejecting them. I'm very ashamed of this part of me, because sometimes I would not know what to do and handle it badly. I want to emphasize that I handled this poorly and I'm not proud of myself for this. All of this has taken me here, almost 10 years later, with no experience whatsoever on any social situation. Right now, I feel very lost in any social event. I don't know how to start a conversation, how to keep it going, how to tell if someone even wants to have that conversation. Like everything, nowadays I have my ups and downs about this. Last year  a uni friend of mine started dating a girl right after I just rejected one. And the worst part is that I liked her. I told myself that it wasn't gonna work and found some excuse to justify myself rejecting her, but ultimately I was just afraid of what could happen. So when my friend started dating this other girl I felt a very very very deep sadness. One that I hadn't felt in a long time. For the next month I remember thinking hourly about this. If I was going to get to that point anytime. Or if I was going to die as an asocial asshole who can't talk to anyone. Don't get me wrong, I was so happy for my guy. He us the most genuine person I have ever met and I'm so happy for him, but that made the difference between us way more apparent and everythung more painful. By this I do not mean thar like I want a girlfriend for the sake of having a girlfriend. I want someone with whom I can connect deeply and talk without restraining myself artificially. It sounds kind of petty, and maybe it is, but it doesn't make it any less painful.

On a kind of related note, another source of anxiety is random love. You probably know what I'm talking about. Falling in love with a girl that told you the time and being infatuated for a couple of days, then feeling bad for not being able to do anything about it. For me the routine has always been like this: fall in love with a classmate/acquaintace, do nothing about it, convince myself that she is not compatible with me for some petty random reason, feel sad and repeat. The process usually took a month max This year it has been different. I have felt something deeper for a girl. Something I wanted to pursue but didn't grow the balls to. I believe you youngsters call it a "crush" haha. And when, eventually (6 months in, ridiculous if you think about it) I found out she was in the process of meeting someone I felt devastated. I wanted to disappear, turn into stone or something. This is the other time I got involuntary suicidal thoughts (at this time a lot of things came together), though I usually dissect them very efficiently because I want to keep living, I know deep down I don't really want to die. The worst part is that she either doesn't have no idea I liked her or thinks I'm some creepy guy. And whenever there's an opportunity to talk to her at a party or something I grt paralyzed thinking she might hate me or stuff like that and end up at square one.

On a totally unrelated note, when i finished highschool I didn't know what I wanted to do. I thought there were 3 ways of feeling good about myself in the future: working on a normal job that let me focus on my hobbies on my free time, working on my passion or working to help other people. The first option seemed like a reliable one, but a sort of pessimistic and utilitarian perspective. The second one sounded cool, but knowing how hard it is to live on an arts related job I ended up discarding it and chose the last option. To clarify, I didn't "settle" for this option, l chose it. So I'm studying medicine and, as you may be thinking, it's pretty hard. Harder that anything I have ever done. Recently I failed 3 whole aubjects and will repeat them next year, which means that I won't be graduating with my peers. This has affected me deeply as it's the first time I failed when I really tried. It was like stumbling upon a wall that everyone has learnt to climb little by little but that I have been neglecting for the past years. It is a weird analogy, sorry. It feels like I have been slacking off for the past 10 years while everyone else has been working hard and now I gotta make up for it.

So here I am. All that has happened to me has made me numb, I'm not able to concentrate, not even on things I like, I'm so insecure that I always seek guidance, even on stuff that I control. This year has been one of the toughest ever, but I have also been trying new things and trying to grow past my old self. I have gone to parties, tried a beer for the first time (I wil stick to coca cola for now haha), found new people... But I can't help but think that it was a stroke of luck and that, the moment I'm in a new environment, I will return to the older state.

I'm afraid of growing resentful of the world, of growing isolated, of growing spiteful, angry and permanently sad. I'm afraid of being a coward who is exaggerating everything and having a main character complex. I'm afraid of being a burden, of being a buzzkill and eventually burning every bridge I have, of people getting tired of me.

Ultimately, I think all of these problems come down to isolation and lack of practice in socian environment and thus have solution, but it's become very hard to be able to do anything at this point, and summer days just go by without anything changing.

Anyways, sorry for the long post

break down daily task to make it less daunting

I found it hard to shower, or get myself to bed when I am anxious or depressed because my brain literally does not work during those times. 

One mental-hack that worked for me: I break down daily tasks to tiny achievable steps such as getting clothes, moving to the bathroom, turning on the shower. I ask myself to do one thing at a time. 

If I don't want to get up, then just put down my phone. If I don't want to shower, I just ask myself to get the clothes out for now. the more steps you take toward it, the more likely you are to do it. If I am not scrolling on my phone, and my clothes are already out, I guess I will just go ahead and shower. 

The same principle applies to other tasks: eating a meal, cleaning a room, doing laundry. Convince yourself you can just do one thing at a time. It is OK to just open the fridge and take a look, throw out this one piece of trash, or take the laundry basket to the laundry room without actually finishing the chore. You will notice once you start moving, it is more likely you will do it eventually. Hope this is helpful.

Hey,

I don't know you personally, and I don't know what you're going through.

Maybe you're having relationship troubles. Maybe someone in your family is having trouble with their health, or they aren't treating you the way you'd like them to. Maybe you're struggling with an addiction, or there's so much in your life that's stressing you out, and you feel like you can't handle it all at once.

Maybe you feel like you've been wronged. Maybe you feel like you aren't enough. Maybe people in your life have told you that, or worse.

Maybe it's none of that. Maybe there's some stuff in your life that just....sucks. Maybe, no matter how hard you try, it feels like you just cannot make progress in the way you want to. Maybe every time you feel like a new opportunity arises, a new door opens, it slams shut in your face.

And on top of that, maybe it feels like there's no one in your life that you can talk to about it. Maybe your family wouldn't understand. Maybe your friends won't listen. Maybe it feels like there isn't anyone, anyone at all, who will listen.

If any of that is true, I'm sure you feel alone, and I'm sorry that you have to go through that :(

Truth be told, I'm not really in the best place either. While I can't say that I know exactly what you're going through, I have experienced at least a little bit of everything I've mentioned so far.

To be clear, this post isn't about me. **This post is about you!** You deserve to be recognized, to be respected, and to be able to vent and openly talk about what you're going through, regardless whether you believe that you deserve it. You **do** deserve it, in case you have any doubts.

But maybe knowing more about me, the person writing this, can help you realize that's there are more people out there that care about you than you think. Maybe knowing about someone else can help you realize that you aren't alone.

👋 **Hi. I'm Ron.** I'm a man in my 20s, and I'm studying for a 4-year degree working with computers. From the outside in, my life seems pretty good. *Really* good, actually.

**Here's a little about me:**

My parents are both still alive and together, and I'm close with both of them. I've never struggled with getting good grades. I have a 2-year degree in acting, and I've been told that I'm especially talented at it by many people who have seen me perform. I can hold a job, and I've even gotten promoted recently. I'm good with money, so much so that I have a digit more in my bank account balance than most of my peers do. I have no problem making friends, and I'm typically on good terms with virtually everyone I know. I have my own car, and I'm on track to be making a lot of money, maybe even six figures a year, within a decade. By all accounts, I'm considered conventionally attractive. That's not me saying that; multiple friends have told me so unprompted, and I have an athletic build from playing sports all my life.

And yet...I still struggle sometimes. With a lot of things, actually; I struggle, or have struggled, with everything on that list.

I love both of my parents, but we occasionally fight. I feel like I can't tell them everything in my life, and I hate that. On top of that, one of my relatives is getting married soon. It's someone I used to be unbelievably close with, closer than nearly anyone else I've ever known. I was asked to be the best man, and I won't even be at the wedding, because this person has warped into someone completely different than the person I was close with.

I get good grades, sure, but that's because I'm a perfectionist. In other words, I spend way too much time on things, because I can't let go unless they're "perfect." They never are.
I'm naturally talented at remembering things, and I got all A's in high school, and because of that, I feel like getting anything below a 95 means that I'm a failure. I got over a 100 average in a semester last year, and I *still* was beating myself up over the fact that I missed a single extra credit question that I felt like I should have known.

I got a degree in acting because people told me that I was good at it and that I should pursue it, and because I didn't know what else to study. For two years and a half years - the extra half year was because I didn't sign up for classes in the right order, another thing I beat myself up over - I studied, got great grades, and was encouraged by people around me. Then the day came when I finally held the degree in my hand, and I remember thinking to myself, "I have no interest at all in doing this with my life." Maybe I wasted two (and a half) years of my life.

I have no problem holding a job, for the most part. But it feels like complacency - regardless whether it is or isn't - because I see other coworkers moving on to other things in life. I got promoted, and the raise felt great. But I got the promotion later than others did, and work tripled in stress during the time when I was learning the ropes.

My finances are pretty good, but I feel guilty for not giving back enough. I have great parents, and I feel like I don't show them that. I have lots of free time, but I never volunteer anywhere.

I communicate well enough, I listen well enough, that I have no problem making friends. But that doesn't always mean I can keep them, or that they're someone that I should keep, if I could. I feel self-conscious about how much I write sometimes, too; just like this post, conversations with my friends often feel like I'm filling up 70% of the space, and that I come across as overbearing but they're too nice to say anything. Some of my friends have distanced themselves from me, and I will sometimes blame myself for that, even with no evidence to support that theory.

I've been told I'm attractive by a few people I know, unprompted. I've been told I have nice eyes, that I have great teeth, that my hairstyle looks great on me, and I know I'm more muscular and athletic than the average person. But I have, for as long as I can remember, never, ever, felt attractive. At best, I've been apathetic. At worse, I've felt insecure, like I could never find someone who would look at me and feel like they wanted to kiss me.

Pretty much every date I've been on has ended the same way: With them saying that the date was good, and that I'm a great guy, but that they didn't want to see me again. One person even kissed me, then texted me later saying that we "weren't a match," with no further explanation. I know it's not the way I acted, since I go out of my way to make sure they were okay with anything that happened. And still, they don't want to see me, and I don't get a specific reason, so I end up blaming my looks, and feeling self-conscious to the point of tears afterwards.

So...**why did I say all of this?**

It's because, as much as I, admittedly, wish I didn't matter sometimes, a wish I'd make so that I could feel better about wanting to check out of all my problems and not look back...**I know that's not true.**

I'm not lying, and I'm not exaggerating: It's genuinely annoying to me sometimes that, deep down, I know that I have value, because it stops me from fully giving in to the feelings of worthlessness that I want to embrace. **And the same is true for you. You. Have. Value.**

Don't believe me? I'll prove it.

Imagine you're in a courtroom. The case being decided is whether you, as a person, matter. Everything you just read, every insecurity I have, was the prosecution talking about my case. But the prosecution isn't the only side in court.

**Now it's time for the defense.**

I've helped people. I see peoples' eyes light up when they see me at my job, because they know I will genuinely listen to them and empathize with their problems. I've heard from the people I manage that I'm their favorite manager they've ever had, because of the way I listen to them, care about their problems, and respect them.

I've given people some of the best gifts they've ever gotten in their life. After my grandmother passed, I made an audio track for my mother of all the voicemails that I secretly saved from my grandmother, including ones where she told my mom that she loved her, and put it to music.

I've given to food banks. Because of me, my local food bank has so much food that they actually encouraged people to take leftovers, because otherwise they would be thrown away.

I might have saved someone's life, who was depressed. She texted me out of the blue years ago, when nothing in her life was going right. I...it feels weird to say that I helped her, but honestly...I did. I didn't feel like I did, but I did, and she told me that I did.

There's more to this list, some things I might not be able to remember, and some things I might not even know.

There is **so much more good** in the world that I can do, for as long as I'm here.

**So. Can. You.**

Did you empathize with any of my insecurities? Maybe.

But let me ask you this: **What about your defense?** If part of your brain is telling you that you don't matter, that you don't have value, you should see if *OTHER* parts of your brain agree.

Think back. What about people you've helped? What about good things you've done for others? Better yet, what about everything that you *could* do? There are people out there whose lives **will** be changed because of good you do.

And here's the best part. The prosecution and the defense are arguing over whether you have value, but again, they aren't the only people in the courtroom. Who's the judge ultimately deciding whether you matter?

**You are.**

My therapist is suggesting I try to reframe negative thoughts.

"I don't feel well, which means it'll travel through my whole family"

"We're always sick. We're going to have to cancel plans again."

"My kids are going to end up in the hospital"

"Everyone gets to enjoy life while we're sick."

I could use some help rephrasing these, or something to repeat to help me through during minor illnesses.

(She’s Filipina) and she knows I’m an emotional wreck so I think when she heard the sadness in my voice she was angry about coming over because it was another sad episode. I got frustrated thinking she wasn’t going to come and then I bartered with God saying if my mom didn’t come over within ten minutes I was going drive to my (old house - I’m selling it, due to a divorce) and parking my car and leaving it on in the garage with the door down. 
 Ultimately, my mom arrived and caught me in my car crying. Similar story around 4 months ago, I planned on taking my whole bottle of Xanax and as I popped the lid off and almost doused it down with water my mother walk through the front door of my apt. 

Needless to say I’m struggling in all aspects of my life.
 1) being the divorce, as my ex husband had an affair with his co worker and who he has been with since. I’ve seen them together outside of our town. I’m conflicted by whether it was my fault for his unfaithfulness. How if I didn’t struggle with both anxiety and depression would this have had happened? I met my ex husband when I was agoraphobic (I didn’t leave my house for four years) he met me in the later half and stuck by me! Through the worst of my anxiety and depression and I wonder if I caused him to be unfaithful. How if I wasn’t such a messed up person, what life would have entailed. At the end of the marriage I struggled with leaving town and not he’s with a girl who is “normal in my eyes.” Who can go out of town without any sort of anxiety, who can do things on a whim without hesitation, and who isn’t always down and depressed. 

2) I’m struggling majorly with feeling alone. I’ve felt this way my entire life as I’m the only child. My parents are the only ones who have been a consistent in my life. All the other family are in another state or live out of the country! I’ve never built a relationship with any of them as they are close to my dad or my mom. It was never about me, it wasn’t about getting to know me. It was always them wanting to talk to either one of my parents and then casually asking how I was doing. 

3) I don’t have friends because I abandon them for fear that I’m not enough. I always feel like they’ll find people better, cooler than me and will realize how big of a loser I am. No one outside of my parents and my mom’s few friends even know my birthday. I’ve never had anyone go all out for my birthday or celebrated it with friends who went all out. There’s always something out there about me that needs fixing. If your loosing hair and gaining weight, check out our cortisol drink or these new breathing techniques, or somatic exercises. I can’t keep up. All this information makes me feel like I have to constantly work on something and it’s a massive overload of my brain. I just want to hide in the woods away from the world and noise. 

5) I have no interest or hobbies. Everyone is always like find a hobby or something you like but all I like is k pop and k dramas. I want to travel the world but my anxiety prevents me from doing it and that gets me down because that’s my dream and then I’m reminded of not having anyone to do this with. I feel like such a loser.

Rejection trauma and feeling like you don't matter. Venting thoughts on a note pad...maybe some can relate.

I've never been special to anyone. I'm always the shadow in the corner. The one who is looked over and ignored. The child playing alone in their room or outside unattended by what I wish was caring parents. The one who slipped through the cracks. So easily discarded or replaced. I don't have a village. Having a village of people who love me fiercely, and find me irreplaceable sounds like a pipe dream. Hitting the lotto would be easier than finding a village like that. Usually those people are your family, friends and maybe a partner. But those things don't exist and never last for me. Cutting Toxic people off felt like a necessary evil. I tell myself I'd rather be alone than treated badly. But once you've cut ties, the darkness sets in. The loneliness takes over and suffocates you. Nobody is chasing you, working to stay in your life. Nobody is eager to change their behavior or apologize for it. So you keep them cut off... The grief that comes with that decision eats at me every day. Why aren't I special? Why haven't I ever been made to feel like me or anything in my life is a big deal? I feel like I'm taking up space and everyone around me, regardless of their significance seems to be bothered by it. What I would give to not feel energy from others or read body language so intensely. To be ignorant or oblivious to other people's feelings towards my presence. Is it because I'm heavy? Is it because I'm ugly? Do I come off rude? Am I too much to handle? Am I really a failure like my mom told me I was? Questions I constantly ask myself. Unfortunately I will never be able to answer these burning questions of mine. To be treated differently and not fully understand the reason. Feeling like the only place I can hide is in my bed with the blinds closed. I belong here. Where nobody can see me, judge me, or become irritated that I'm in their presence. Being locked away is what everyone wants it seems. So I oblige... The cycle continues until I spiral into deep disparity. I have nowhere to expel these deep rooted feelings, and I feel like I'm going to explode from the inside out. I'm so confused. Why didn't my graduation get celebrated? Why was I the one ignored as a kid? Why wasn't my sweet 16 a big deal? Why didn't my mom throw me a baby shower? Why am I failure for being chronically ill? Why does what I have to offer never seem to be enough? Why do people make me feel this way and then punish me by making me feel like the crazy one when I lash out emotionally? Why aren't my boundaries respected? Why would people rather lose me than respect me and fix things? I just don't understand. I pour my heart into people, give them love and understanding in all the ways I wish people would give me. And I'm thrown away, like a used napkin or or a piece of overlooked junk mail. I'm not special to anyone and I never will be. At least not forever. Maybe for the season. But that's what it is. A chapter, a cycle, or a stand in until there's something better. Why can't people see my beauty. Yeah, I'm quirky, moody, and slow to learn... But I'm also really fricking smart. I don't want to conform to others' ideas of who I should be. And there lies part of the problem. Being my true self comes with the consequence of losing it all. And all this pain has caused me to become sensitive, difficult, and angry. And now that I'm all these things, nobody wants to handle it. But they are the ones who caused this pain... So why am I being punished? When I see people with big close families and big friend groups, and a loving partner I think to myself "wow, this person has hit the jackpot in life!!" And the envy consumes me until I'm deeply sad from comparing my situation to theirs. I feel like silence is the answer. Staying out of the way is the answer. If I'm not seen or heard, nobody can hurt me... right? So I have a defense mechanism... I guess it's always been my weapon of choice. To be a sensitive person with big feelings, is to become a tortured soul and a forever discarded napkin in the trash can.

How to deal with a parent in denial 

Hello, everyone. Here’s my situation, and I’m stuck and don’t know what to do. 

My grandma (84) has an end stage cancer. She’s on hospice care at home, which in my country means that an oncologist visits her once a month and calls her once a week to check how she tolerates prescribed painkillers. She’s not entirely bed bound yet, she’s able to go to a toilet herself and to heat up food. She barely walks around her tiny flat and can’t take a shower by herself. Painkillers make her sleepy which is normal, and pain management is the most important part of the hospice care. 

Here’s the problem. My father is her primary caregiver. He visits her once a week and brings her food. On Thursday she slipped, fell and couldn’t stand up on her own. She has to wait for him to come and help her. He lives 25 miles away, and it’s a big city, so with a crazy traffic, it can take him two hours to get to her in case of an emergency. 

Now, he’s in denial about grandma’s condition. 14 months ago grandma developed symptoms that strongly suggested it was cancer recurrence. She didn’t believe me, dad didn’t believe me and for almost six months he was telling me that she had invented all the symptoms including pain. Last summer she had seen an oncology team, was diagnosed with recurrence and had a round of palliative chemo last autumn. In the beginning of winter she has quickly deteriorated and become disabled. 

Dad is sure that grandma can continue living alone. He refuses to hire a nurse, he’s scared of getting her into a hospice for better care, he was against opioid painkillers even though it was obvious that grandma needed them a month ago already. He’s certain she has years to live and that she will die soon if she looses her independence. 

Now, because of her age and her disease, gramma is slightly delusional. She can still talk all right, but her short memory is basically nonexistent. She denies her cancer and thinks her symptoms are explained by Covid, gastritis, etc. I don’t have problems with her interpretation, because she’s too old. 

She struggles with taking meds by schedule, she doesn’t understand what her oncologists tells her. Dad doesn’t speak with her doctors. 

To me it’s obvious she needs more help. Dad even refused to give a spare pair of keys to the neighbors in case of another emergency. He thinks that grandma should give the keys to the neighbor herself, and to me it sounds fucking insane. 

I understand that he’s scared of losing his mother, but his denial is out of hand. 

I tried using logic to convince him. He just keeps arguing with me and keeps telling that I’m over reacting. I tried not to discuss this situation at all with him (I call grandma a lot and try to support her). But when another bad thing happens to grandma, he texts me asking me what he shall do. Then he refuses to take any action. We had a bad verbal fight last summer, but again, it was completely useless in the long run. 

This is not a first situation he’s behaving in such a manner. Twenty years ago another close family member was very (mentally) sick, I was 15 then and I witnessed a full blown psychosis and my dad said that it wasn’t that bad, etc. I left home on the day I turned 18. I’ve developed cPTSD after the first situation, and the mentioned mentally ill family member lives in the next house to grandma. Which makes it impossible for me to visit grandma and help her myself (I live in another city).

I’m stumped. I know that grandma won’t live forever, and I thought that every person deserves to spend the last months in comfort and with dignity. I don’t find the current situation even safe. 

Is there anything I can try to make my father understand? I guess, my other option is to simply accept the way the things are and that I’m powerless. 

I’ve got a therapist but I can’t afford seeing her for at least another months (I’ve just got my car maintained and my dog has a surgery, so I’m broke). 

Thanks everyone who has read till the end. 

tl;dr. Grandma has an end stage cancer and dad is denial and refuses to get her help.
